title: Statement Regarding MF Global Inc.

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York suspended MF Global Inc. from conducting new business on October 31, 2011, pending MF Global's ability to demonstrate it can satisfy the NY Fed's primary dealer requirements. The suspension remained in effect until MF Global either proved compliance or the NY Fed decided to terminate its primary dealer status.
key_points: MF Global Inc. was suspended from conducting new business with the Federal Reserve Bank of New York on October 31, 2011.
The suspension remained in effect until MF Global demonstrated it could satisfy the requirements of the NY Fed's policy 'Administration of Relationships with Primary Dealers.'
The NY Fed reserved the right to terminate MF Global's primary dealer status entirely.
This statement predates MF Global's Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing, which occurred later the same day.
The suspension relates to MF Global's role as a primary dealer in the Treasury market.
why_it_matters: This statement marks a critical moment in the MF Global crisis. The suspension of a primary dealer from Federal Reserve operations signaled severe operational or financial distress at a major market participant. Primary dealers are essential intermediaries in Treasury market operations, and their suspension affects market liquidity infrastructure.
market_structure_context: The suspension directly implicates the primary dealer system, a core component of Treasury market structure. Primary dealers serve as counterparties to the Federal Reserve in open market operations and play a key role in Treasury auction functionality. This suspension preceded MF Global's bankruptcy by hours, illustrating how primary dealer status revocation functions as an early warning signal of dealer insolvency.
notes: This is a historical record from October 31, 2011—the same day MF Global filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. The source is directly relevant to market structure through its implications for the primary dealer system. However, the source text is very thin, providing only the bare-bones statement without additional context about why the suspension was imposed or what specific deficiencies were identified. Lowered confidence slightly due to age and source thinness.
